text.title: project-docs-convention Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for project-docs-convention as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: If CLAUDE.md contains anything other than `@AGENTS.md`, Claude Code will load mixed content — the @-ref pattern is strict: one line only.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any extra content breaks the pattern. Use `printf "@AGENTS.md\n" > CLAUDE.md` to ensure exact format.

failure-mode.description: AGENTS.md with @-refs to other files. Standalone agents do not follow @-refs; they read AGENTS.md directly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AGENTS.md must be fully self-contained. Put cross-references in .agents/INDEX.md instead.

failure-mode.description: INDEX.md tables without clear descriptions. Agents scanning for "where is X?" need one-line summaries per entry.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Detailed docs go in the referenced files; INDEX.md is only a map.

failure-mode.description: Confusing `.product/` (per-project SDD) with `.aidocs/` (workspace-level SDD). Different purposes, different content.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Document the mapping explicitly in the root AGENTS.md so agents know which to use.

failure-mode.description: Agents creating new directories without creating CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md pairs. The pair creation must be explicit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always include pair creation in agent task prompts. Standard template: CLAUDE.md = `@AGENTS.md`, AGENTS.md = 20-80 lines describing the directory.
